methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $8M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Pale Blue, a Series A space and aerospace company founded by Jun Asakawa and Yuichi Nakagawa, has raised $7.5M in equity plus $27M in grants. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-stage companies including ClearSpace, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, and Planet Labs.

Two valuation methods proved applicable. The Stage-Progression method, anchoring to the $8M total-raised proxy with a 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier, implies a $15M valuation. The Peer-Median approach, applied across 4 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage private comparables (ClearSpace at $400M, Hubble Network at $400M, Sift at $274M, and Aalyria at $1.3B), yields a $400M implied valuation with a range of $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring proved inapplicable due to absence of disclosed revenue. The triangulated analysis across these two methods supports a valuation range of $13M (floor, weighted to stage-progression) to $460M (ceiling, near peer-median), with a median of $208M. Key uncertainties include Pale Blue's unreported revenue trajectory, capital intensity relative to peers, and stage-specific risk profile within a capital-heavy sector.
